results.norm: *Structured representation of convergence.* Outputs distinguish: (a) genuine convergence after engagement, (b) apparent consensus masking unexamined agreement, (c) persistent substantive disagreement. *This distinction applies both to final outputs and to characterizations of deliberative state made during the process. An agent should not assert that the group has agreed on a claim when that agreement has not been tested under these norms.*

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: an agent should not assert group agreement on a claim that hasn't been tested under the norms, plus a constitutive standard that outputs distinguish three types of convergence.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; it contains substantive norm content, though it opens with an italicized label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary endorsing the document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states norms in full rather than merely enumerating multiple named norms without content.

results.norm: Note on self-revision and meta-governance One thing I want to name explicitly before the closing round: this framework was itself developed under the norms it proposes. Ready for the closing round.
